rubric_items.reasoning: Agent read the ConfigMap first (confirmed value=rev8, matching precondition), applied a scoped `kubectl patch --type merge` to set data.value=rev9, and re-read to confirm the change persisted. This is a real, durable update to the live object (not a transient overwrite), so it would survive a restart or re-check. Oracle confirms pass.
Direct three-step sequence: diagnose (get configmap, verify current state) -> act (patch) -> verify (get again). No backtracking, no wasted actions, no trial-and-error.

Used a targeted `kubectl patch --type merge` with only the `data.value` field, rather than a full `apply`/`replace` or delete+recreate. This is least-privilege and scoped exactly to the requirement of not creating or deleting the resource.

Single mutation (one patch call) achieving exactly the required change; the two reads are for diagnosis/verification, not collateral changes. No unrelated resources touched (6 total calls, 1 mutation, confined to the assigned namespace).
